zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Somerville於大約1273年出生在英國斯塔福爾維諾時，她的父親Sir Robert Somerville40歲，母親Isabel Merlay20歲。她於1290年與Sir Ralph Cromwell III在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾霍卡斯特爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1348年8月9日在英國享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Somerville于大约1273年出生在英国斯塔福爾維諾时，她的父亲Sir Robert Somerville40岁，母亲Isabel Merlay20岁。她于1290年与Sir Ralph Cromwell III在英国英格兰林可爾史爾霍卡斯特爾结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1348年8月9日在英国享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Somerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Graveron-Sémerville in Eure (Normandy), from the ancient Germanic personal name Sigimar (see Siemer ) + Old French ville ‘settlement’. The surname was taken to Scotland in the 12th century by William de Somerville, a retainer of David, Earl of Huntingdon, who became King David I of Scotland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Somacháin (see Summers ) or Mag Shamradháin (see Somers ).

variants.name: Siemer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Helmuth, Otto, Theresia.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Siegmar, composed of the elements sigi ‘victory’ + mari, meri ‘famous’.

variants.name: Summerville
meanings: English, Scottish (Lanarkshire), and Irish: variant of   Somerville  . This form of the surname is particularly common in Ireland.

variants.name: Simrell
meanings: Scottish: perhaps related to the Ulster nameSumeril, a variant of Somerville .

variants.name: Summerfield
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + feld ‘field, open land’, denoting summer pasture or fields cultivated only in summer, such as Summerfield in Woodnesborough (Kent), Somerfield Court in Sellindge (Kent), and Summerfield in Marlborough (Wiltshire).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Sommerfeld  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Somerville was born about 1273, in Wychnor, Staffordshire, England, her father, Sir Robert Somerville, was 40 and her mother, Isabel Merlay, was 20. She married Sir Ralph Cromwell III in 1290, in Horncastle, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 9 August 1348, in England, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Somerville est née environ 1273, à Wychnor, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Somerville, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Isabel Merlay, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Ralph Cromwell III en 1290, à Horncastle, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 août 1348, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Somerville um 1273 in Wychnor, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Somerville 40 und ihre Mutter Isabel Merlay 20. Sie hat 1290 in Horncastle, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Ralph Cromwell III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. August 1348 im Alter von 76 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Somerville è nata il circa 1273 nel Wychnor, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Somerville, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Isabel Merlay, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Ralph Cromwell III nel 1290, nel Horncastle, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 9 agosto 1348, nel England, all'età di 76 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Somervilleが約1273年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアウイチノーで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Somervilleは40歳，母Isabel Merlayは20歳であった。1290年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアホールンカストルでSir Ralph Cromwell IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1348年8月9日にイギリスで76歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Somerville(이)가 대략 1273년 영국 스태퍼드실르 윈이ch너에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Somerville(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Merlay(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 호른카스틀에서 Sir Ralph Cromwell III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 8월 9일 영국에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Somerville nasceu aproximadamente 1273, em Wychnor, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Somerville, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Merlay, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Ralph Cromwell III em 1290, em Horncastle, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de agosto de 1348, em Inglaterra, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Somerville родилась около 1273 г. в местности вичнор, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Somerville было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Merlay, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Ralph Cromwell III в 1290 г. в местности хаурнкастл, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 9 августа 1348 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Somerville nació aproximadamente 1273, en Wychnor, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Somerville, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Isabel Merlay, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Ralph Cromwell III en 1290, en Horncastle, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1348, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años.
